Article Title: "I was petrified and determined": Alix Earle opened up about getting humiliated in the bedroom and what she did to turn it all around
Article Text:
Alix Earle (Getty Images)

What did Alix Earle say about her ex-boyfriend’s bedroom criticism?

Influencer and reality star Alix Earle has stirred debate after revealing on a podcast that an early relationship critique pushed her to improve her sexual confidence and skills. Speaking on Call Her Daddy, she recalled a blunt comment from an ex-boyfriend that she says changed how she approached intimacy. The discussion quickly spread online, pulling in reactions far beyond her usual social media audience and even linking her name again with NFL circles and past rumors involving high-profile athletes in recent days.Alix Earle said during the podcast:“The first one I gave a blowjob to, whatever... He was like, ‘That was the worst thing I’ve ever experienced in my entire life.’ ... So I was petrified and determined, so I studied.”She told Cooper she responded to that early embarrassment with sheer determination, studying until she felt genuinely confident.Alix Earle’s name has repeatedly surfaced in conversations around NFL figures, though she has not confirmed any serious relationship with them. She was previously linked to Houston Texans wide receiver Braxton Berrios, and more recently spotted in the same social orbit as former quarterback Tom Brady at multiple events.From New Year’s celebrations where they were seen dancing to a Super Bowl party in February, speculation followed each appearance. However, much of it remains unverified, driven more by social media moments than confirmed reporting.In recent interviews, Earle has hinted at shifting her dating preferences, even joking that she is stepping away from football players and now interested in a rock star. Her past relationship with Braxton Berrios continues to draw attention, especially after the two reportedly crossed paths again at a Miami Formula 1 event, where reactions were noted but not detailed publicly. Meanwhile, Brady’s personal life remains in the spotlight following his divorce from Gisele Bündchen, who has since remarried and welcomed a child. Brady himself has stayed single, with occasional headlines from public appearances, including a light moment during a Netflix roast involving Kevin Hart’s wife.The overlap between celebrity culture, sports fame and social media continues to amplify even casual remarks into broader storylines. Earle’s comments, originally shared in a podcast setting, quickly moved beyond entertainment spaces into sports discussions because of her past connections. While she has not framed her personal life around public validation, the attention reflects how closely influencer culture now intersects with professional athletes and their public images. For Earle, the focus remains on her own narrative, even as outside speculation keeps reshaping it. That dynamic ensures her name stays in circulation across both entertainment and sports media.
Article Summary: Alix Earle says an early relationship critique pushed her to improve her sexual confidence. The discussion quickly spread online, pulling in reactions far beyond her usual social media audience. Her name has repeatedly surfaced in conversations around NFL figures, though she has not confirmed any serious relationship with them.

Article Title: “Troy would call the police”: Michael Irvin reveals why Troy Aikman threatened to call police during wild Cowboys house parties in the 1990s
Article Text:
Michael Irvin and Troy Aikman (Getty Images)

Was Troy Aikman really different from the Cowboys’ party culture?

What Was the Dallas Cowboys' 'White House' and How Did It Start?

The Dallas Cowboys of the 1990s were two very different teams rolled into one. On the field, they were ruthless and disciplined, one of the most dominant franchises the NFL had ever seen. Off it, a good number of them lived by completely different rules. Wide receiver Michael Irvin , who recently opened up about those years on his podcast The White House with Michael Irvin, summed it all up with one story about Troy Aikman that you genuinely could not make up.When asked which player never once showed up to the infamous "White House," Irvin didn't skip a beat. "Troy, Troy never came," he said. And then came the reason."Troy was different. Troy Aikman, dude, I'm telling you, I've never seen someone so mature at a young age, man," Irvin said. "Troy, he was he's always been that way, man. I mean, we were winning Super Bowls. He would come home, and he would have naked women out of his pool already having a party waiting on him. And Troy would call the police on him."The locker room reaction the next day was one of pure disbelief. "Dude, this true story, dude," Irvin added, laughing. "He comes in that locker room, the next thing we were like, what the f***? You had all our numbers. You didn't call anyone else before you called the police? What are you doing?"ID@undefinedCaption not available.ID@undefinedCaption not available.For Aikman, though, this was entirely consistent with who he was. During the season, he stuck to a strict routine, studied his playbook, and kept a low profile. He'd reportedly wind down watching Jay Leno before bed rather than heading out. Off the field, he quietly visited sick children and was awarded the 1997 NFL Man of the Year for his work with the Troy Aikman Foundation, which built playrooms in children's hospitals. Teammate Darren Woodson, who played alongside him from 1992 to 2000, put it simply: "When you talk about leadership skills, you talk about controlling the huddle... [Aikman] is probably the most accurate quarterback I've ever seen throw the ball."While Troy Aikman was calling the cops, his teammates were busy building something of a legend nearby. The "White House" was a two-story party house near the Cowboys' Valley Ranch headquarters, and Irvin considered himself its president. It came to life after Jerry Jones replaced strict head coach Jimmy Johnson with Barry Switzer, whose relaxed approach gave players a freedom they had never had before. "Barry came into that locker room, the dynamic, the accountability, the work ethic, that had changed," former safety James Washington recalled. Switzer himself said what players did off the field was "none of our business."Irvin recalled the logic behind buying the place: "We were like, 'Man, we spend a lot of money for these hotels, all of these women.' So then we said, 'Let's do a house.'" The house reportedly had five separate rooms, each with its own scene and atmosphere.
Article Summary: Michael Irvin opened up about the Dallas Cowboys' 'White House' on his podcast. He revealed that quarterback Troy Aikman never showed up to the party. Irvin: "He would come home, and he would have naked women out of his pool"

Article Title: Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel controversy turns into monetization battle, according to insider: "People are out there digging"
Article Text:
Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel (Getty Images)

Mike Florio says Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini situation being exploited for financial incentive online

New details about New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel and journalist Dianna Russini aren't slowing down. TMZ reported that Russini was visibly pregnant during a private boat rental she shared with Vrabel at a Tennessee lake in June 2021, the same period she covered the Tennessee Titans, the team Vrabel was then coaching. A video of the two at the marina surfaced the following day, adding fuel to a story that shows no signs of cooling.The newest reporting sparked another discussion on NBC Sports’ Pro Football Talk, where Mike Florio suggested the story may still be far from over. Florio pointed to how quickly new material surfaced this week and argued that the unpredictable nature of the situation is exactly what makes it difficult for anyone involved to get ahead of it.“In a story where we’ve had no way of expecting whatever is coming next… You have photos of the two of them at a marina at some lake in Tennessee from June 2021, and then the next day, a video of an extremely pregnant Dianna Russini walking down the dock with Mike Vrabel,” Florio said. “Who would’ve expected that? And that just proves the point: expect the unexpected.”Florio also focused on the growing media frenzy around the situation.According to him, the continued leaks are being fueled by people actively searching through old material, hoping to uncover more evidence connected to the pair.“People are out there digging through anything they can find,” Florio added. “People are looking through their phones… There’s a financial incentive for TMZ in the New York Post to get anything they can. And the point is, we never know what is going to be that one thing that just makes this untenable.”So far, the fallout has looked very different for both sides. Russini resigned from The Athletic in April, yet fresh leaks and speculation continue to follow her. Vrabel, meanwhile, has largely avoided major professional consequences. He missed Day 3 of the 2026 NFL Draft but returned shortly afterward for the Patriots’ offseason program.The NFL itself appears unlikely to formally step into the matter since it remains a personal issue rather than a league investigation. Teams were reportedly allowed to decide for themselves whether to reference the situation in schedule release videos, and the Los Angeles Chargers wasted little time doing exactly that ahead of their matchup with the New England Patriots.For now, the story remains active because the stream of material has not stopped. That uncertainty, more than anything else, is what keeps the controversy alive around Vrabel and Russini.
Article Summary: Mike Florio says Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini situation being exploited for financial incentive online. Florio: The unpredictable nature of the situation is exactly what makes it difficult for anyone involved to get ahead of it. The NFL itself appears unlikely to formally step into the matter.

Article Title: "He’s throwing everything he can": Mike Vrabel reportedly battling to save marriage as new Dianna Russini scandal details keep emerging
Article Text:
Mike Vrabel is reportedly doing “whatever it takes” to save his 27-year marriage after the ongoing controversy involving Dianna Russini. Sources claim Vrabel is attending both solo and couples therapy with wife Jen while trying to repair trust behind the scenes. Meanwhile, Russini and her husband are also reportedly presenting a united front as the scandal continues dominating NFL headlines and social media discussion.

Mike Vrabel reportedly determined to save marriage after Dianna Russini controversy

Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel scandal continues shaking NFL world

Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini continue to dominate NFL headlines, but behind the scenes the focus has turned to the personal damage done in the aftermath of this. Amid daily speculation on social media, new reports suggest Vrabel is now almost entirely focused on saving his marriage after weeks of public scrutiny.The Patriots coach has reportedly committed himself to counseling and rebuilding trust with wife Jen Vrabel following the controversy that erupted earlier this spring. Sources close to the situation claim the longtime couple is trying to move forward privately despite relentless public attention surrounding the scandal.According to insiders, Vrabel has taken the situation seriously from the start and understands the pressure now surrounding his family.“Mike is fully committed to making sure his marriage weathers this storm,” a source told Globe. “He’s in therapy solo and with Jen, that’s a non-negotiable. This isn’t something you can solve overnight, but he’s throwing everything he can at this.”The controversy intensified after photos and videos surfaced allegedly showing Vrabel and Russini together in intimate situations over multiple years. Both parties denied wrongdoing, but the images quickly became one of the NFL’s biggest offseason talking points.Vrabel later addressed the emotional toll publicly while speaking about his decision to pursue counseling.“As I said the other day, I promised my family, this organization and this team that I was going to give them the best version of me that I can possibly give them,” the three-time Super Bowl champion announced on Wednesday, April 22, per ESPN. “I have always wanted to lead by example, and I believe this is what I have to do to be the best husband, father and coach that I possibly can be. This is not an easy thing for me to admit, but it is one that I know will make me a better person. I appreciate the support that everyone has given me and promise a stronger resolve as a result.”While Vrabel reportedly works on repairing his family life, Russini is also facing major personal and professional consequences.The former Athletic insider resigned from her role amid the ongoing controversy, though sources insist she and husband Kevin Goldschmidt are trying to remain united publicly and privately.“By all accounts Dianna is just as all-in on her marriage,” explained the source. “It’s clear they’re making a big effort to put on a very united front.”The insider added, “That being said they have always had a reputation for being one of those couples that is very affectionate in public. That’s why this whole thing has been extra shocking for anyone that knows them, no one would have predicted this.”Despite the noise surrounding the scandal, sources close to Vrabel reportedly believe he remains confident his marriage can survive the storm.
Article Summary: Mike Vrabel is reportedly doing “whatever it takes” to save his 27-year marriage after the ongoing controversy involving Dianna Russini. Sources claim the longtime couple is trying to move forward privately despite relentless public attention surrounding the scandal. Meanwhile, Russini and her husband are also reportedly presenting a united front.

Article Title: "She has to be stopped": Dianna Russini faces new accusations as Mike Vrabel scandal explodes again
Article Text:
Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel remain at the centre of NFL controversy after fresh public photos reignited online debate. Critics accused Russini of attempting damage control while questions surrounding her alleged relationship with Vrabel continue dominating headlines. The backlash intensified after harsh comments described her recent behaviour as a “sign of desperation,” further dividing fans across social media as the scandal continues overshadowing football discussions.

Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel controversy sparks explosive reaction

Mike Vrabel scandal continues overshadowing NFL headlines

The controversy surrounding Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel refuses to slow down. Just when attention around the scandal appeared to calm slightly, new public photos involving Russini and her husband reignited debate across the NFL world and social media.The latest images showed Russini sharing a kiss with husband Kevin Goldschmidt outside their home while holding one of their children. While some viewed the moment as a sign the family was trying to move forward privately, others believed it looked carefully staged amid the continuing backlash tied to Vrabel.Former NFL MVP Cam Newton became one of the loudest critics after the photos surfaced online. During his “4th&1” podcast, Newton openly questioned the timing and intent behind the public appearance.“I’m saying to myself, hold on, Diana, you need to stop it,” Newton said. “You need to stop. She has to be stopped.”Newton then referenced Russini’s son being named Michael, a detail that has fueled endless internet speculation because of Vrabel’s first name. Russini has reportedly maintained the name honors her brother, not the Patriots coach, but that explanation has not stopped online theories from spreading.“This is a sign of desperation, this is the old Hail Mary Doug Fluty,” Newton added. “And out of all the photographers and the pictures… Kevin, this got to be AI [the picture].”The comments instantly exploded across social media. Some users agreed with Newton’s criticism, arguing the public display felt overly calculated. Others pushed back hard, accusing critics of crossing personal boundaries and turning a private family matter into public entertainment.The scandal has continued dominating football conversations for weeks, even after the NFL Draft and schedule release shifted attention back toward the upcoming season.The controversy originally intensified after photos surfaced showing Russini and Vrabel together at a luxury Arizona resort. Additional images and videos from previous years later emerged online, including footage from bars, vacations, and boat trips, fueling even more speculation about the nature of their relationship.Both Russini and Vrabel denied wrongdoing. Vrabel reportedly called the allegations “laughable,” while Russini stated the gatherings involved groups of friends and professional interactions outside the workplace.Still, the fallout has been difficult to ignore. Russini eventually resigned from her senior insider role at The Athletic amid internal investigations, while Vrabel stepped back from certain Patriots responsibilities to focus on counseling and family matters.Newton also questioned whether forgiveness should come easily after public embarrassment. “The same way that you embarrass me, the same way I need you to apologize,” he added. “That’s just me. That’s why it’s questionable. But are you sorry because that’s how you really feel? Or are you sorry because you got caught?”
Article Summary: Dianna Russini and husband Kevin Goldschmidt share a kiss outside their home while holding one of their children. Critics accused Russini of attempting damage control while questions surrounding her alleged relationship with Vrabel continue dominating headlines. Former NFL MVP Cam Newton became one of the loudest critics after the photos surfaced online.

Article Title: Patrick Mahomes’ ACL injury sparks panic as Andy Reid urged to overhaul Kansas City Chiefs offense
Article Text:
Patrick Mahomes remains the biggest concern for the Kansas City Chiefs heading into the 2026 season after suffering a torn ACL and LCL last year. A former Super Bowl champion warned Andy Reid to rely more on the run game instead of putting immediate pressure on Mahomes after surgery. Although the Chiefs believe the quarterback is ahead of schedule for Week 1, growing injury fears continue dominating NFL discussions surrounding Kansas City’s Super Bowl hopes.

Patrick Mahomes ACL injury sparks concern over Chiefs offense

Andy Reid faces pressure as Patrick Mahomes recovery timeline improves

The Kansas City Chiefs are entering the 2026 season with far more uncertainty than usual. For the first time in years, the franchise is preparing for life after a serious injury to Patrick Mahomes , and not everyone believes the team can simply return to business as normal.Concerns around Mahomes’ recovery continue growing after the quarterback suffered a torn ACL and LCL against the Los Angeles Chargers last December. Now, a former Super Bowl champion is publicly warning Andy Reid that Kansas City may need to completely rethink its offensive identity before the season begins.Speaking on ESPN’s “Get Up,” former NFL star Damien Woody made it clear he believes Kansas City cannot place the same workload on Mahomes immediately after his recovery."There's not a better time for that to happen than now, because your quarterback is coming off an ACL," Damien Woody, a two-time Super Bowl winner and ESPN analyst, said on Thursday on ESPN's "Get Up."Woody argued that Reid must lean more heavily on the running game instead of asking Mahomes to carry the offense from Week 1. That opinion has gained traction because the Chiefs already appear to be preparing for a philosophical shift.Kansas City strengthened its backfield this offseason by signing Kenneth Walker III, who enters 2026 with enormous momentum after earning Super Bowl MVP honours earlier this year. The move immediately fueled speculation that the Chiefs may finally commit to a more balanced offensive attack.The concern surrounding Mahomes is understandable. Kansas City struggled badly after the injury, collapsing to a 6-11 finish and missing the playoffs for the first time since 2014. Suddenly, the aura of invincibility around the franchise disappeared.Despite the anxiety, there are signs the organization remains optimistic about Mahomes’ recovery.According to ESPN insider Adam Schefter, the Chiefs internally believe the star quarterback is progressing faster than expected ahead of their Monday Night Football opener against the Denver Broncos."The league doesn't do that unless they think there's a realistic chance that Patrick Mahomes is going to be back," ESPN's Adam Schefter said on "Get Up.""The feeling right now within the organization is that he is tracking to be ready for that Monday night game against Bo Nix, who is coming off his own surgeries on his ankle, and the Denver Broncos in Kansas City."Chiefs general manager Brett Veach has reportedly described Mahomes as “way ahead of schedule,” while Reid recently hinted the quarterback could participate in offseason activities.Still, the bigger question remains unanswered. Even if Mahomes returns by Week 1, can Kansas City realistically expect him to survive another season playing the same aggressive style immediately after major knee surgery? That debate may shape the entire NFL season.
Article Summary: Patrick Mahomes remains the biggest concern for the Kansas City Chiefs heading into the 2026 season. A former Super Bowl champion warned Andy Reid to rely more on the run game instead of putting immediate pressure on Mahomes after surgery. Kansas City struggled badly after the injury, collapsing to a 6-11 finish and missing the playoffs.

Article Title: “This is a sign of desperation”: Dianna Russini’s appearance with husband takes unexpected turn amid Mike Vrabel drama

Dianna Russini’s appearance with husband takes unexpected turn amid Mike Vrabel drama. (Image via Getty Images)

Dianna Russini and her husband stay strong amid disturbing rumors of her affair with Mike Vrabel

Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini might have remained distant from each other in public ever since their pictures from the Arizona hotel were leaked, but their affair rumors have refused to die down.Just a few days ago, the former NFL insider and reporter of the New York Times’ Dianna Russini sparked shockwaves across the league as she was spotted kissing her husband outside her home in New Jersey.A few hours ago, former NFL player Cam Newton made an appearance on the “4th & 1” podcast where he opened up about how Dianna Russini’s pictures with her husband, Kevin Goldschmidt, reek of “desperation”.Cam Newton said, “This is a sign of desperation, this is the old Hail Mary Doug Fluty…”The former player added, “And out of all the photographers and the pictures… Kevin, this got to be AI [the picture].”Cam Newton continued, “I’m saying to myself, hold on, Diana, you need to stop it…You need to stop. She has to be stopped.”However, neither Dianna Russini nor her husband has responded to the speculations about their new pictures yet as they try to remain out of the spotlight.Over the last few weeks, Dianna Russini and her marriage have dominated headlines as millions were convinced she is facing issues in her marital life.Millions also believed that her husband, Kevin Goldschmidt, has been looking for ways to end their marriage.But it seems the reality is far from truth. Article Title: Did Steve Kerr secretly hide Taylor Swift lyrics in his interviews? Warriors coach finally reveals the truth
Article Text:
Taylor Swift and Steve Kerr. Image via: Getty Images

Steve Kerr admits to secretly hiding Taylor Swift lyrics in his interviews



Taylor Swift. Image via: Gareth Cattermole— TAS Rights Management/Getty Images

Steve Kerr addresses return to the Warriors



Steve Kerr has handled pressure-packed playoff interviews, championship celebrations and difficult seasons across more than a decade with the Golden State Warriors. Yet one of his most unexpected coaching habits involved quietly slipping Taylor Swift lyrics into postgame press conferences while nobody noticed. What started as a private joke eventually reached Swift herself, leaving even the pop superstar surprised by how far Kerr had taken it.The revelation surfaced during a recent ESPN feature, where Kerr admitted he spent months weaving lines from Swift’s hit song “All Too Well” into his media sessions during the 2022-23 NBA season. One of the earliest examples came after a win over Houston in March 2023, when Kerr casually opened his remarks by saying, “I walked through the door of the locker room at halftime,” echoing the opening line from the song. At the time, nobody in the room caught on.According to Steve Kerr, the idea slowly turned into a season-long challenge. He kept finding ways to work more lyrics into interviews while maintaining a straight face, all without reporters or fans realizing what he was doing.Eventually, his son Matthew pieced the clips together into a single edited video and shared it inside the family group chat. The final result reportedly showed Kerr nearly reciting the entire song over the course of the season through scattered interview answers.The prank became even more surreal when the video somehow found its way to Swift through a mutual friend. Kerr recalled the singer reacting with disbelief.“Wait, is this real?” Swift asked after watching the compilation.Later, she reportedly followed up with another request. “Can I put it on social media?” she asked. Kerr declined, saying Swift described the joke as “creative and funny,” but the full video has still not been released publicly.The story offered a lighter glimpse into a coach often associated with intensity and championship expectations. Still, Kerr’s week was not only about hidden song lyrics and viral stories.Kerr also spoke openly about his decision to return as Warriors head coach after signing a two-year extension. The 60-year-old admitted he seriously considered retirement before ultimately deciding he could not walk away from the organization.“My wife said, ‘You might coach again someday, but you’ll never coach the Warriors again,’” Kerr explained. “And that was really meaningful because I love this team, I love our players and that struck me.”After Golden State finished 37-45 and missed the playoffs, Kerr acknowledged he must improve next season. “I know I have to be better. I didn’t have a great coaching year,” he admitted.The veteran coach pointed toward turnovers, discipline and the need for younger players as priorities moving forward. “We need some younger legs, for sure,” Kerr said, as the Warriors prepare for another important offseason around Stephen Curry and Draymond Green.
Article Summary: Steve Kerr admitted he spent months weaving lines from Taylor Swift’s hit song “All Too Well” into his media sessions during the 2022-23 NBA season. He kept finding ways to work more lyrics into interviews while maintaining a straight face. The prank became even more surreal when the video somehow found its way to Swift through a mutual friend.

Article Title: New court update emerges in Miles Bridges’ dispute with ex over alleged cyberstalking
Article Text:
Miles Bridges. Image via: Rusty Jones/ AP

Miles Bridges’ courtroom battle with his ex intensifies after cyberstalking accusations



Miles Bridges. Image via: Getty Images

Legal tensions continue as both sides deny key allegations



Miles Bridges and his former partner, Mychelle Johnson, are once again at the center of a growing legal dispute, with fresh court filings adding another layer to a case that has already drawn significant public attention. Days after Bridges secured a temporary restraining order, Johnson formally responded to several allegations made against her, denying claims tied to cyberstalking, harassment, and alleged attempts to damage the Charlotte Hornets forward’s vehicle.The latest filings paint two sharply different versions of the same night. Bridges accused Johnson of leaving their children with a nanny and showing up at his March 21 game at the Spectrum Center with the intent to tamper with his car. Johnson, however, said she attended because a friend had an extra ticket and insisted there was no plan to interfere with Bridges or his property. What began as another tense chapter in a difficult relationship has now evolved into a broader courtroom battle involving accusations of intimidation, manipulation, and emotional distress.According to court documents first obtained by TMZ, Johnson admitted she attended the Hornets game on Bridges’ birthday but denied the allegations surrounding her visit.She claimed the ticket came through a friend connected to a player on the opposing team and said she chose not to tell Bridges because “he does not like her getting involved with other men.”Johnson also disputed claims that she abandoned their four children to attend the game or attempted to damage Bridges’ tires. Her attorney, Ginny Conley, strongly rejected the accusations in a court response.“These allegations are false, without merit, without proof, made up and are designed by the Father and his attorney to harass the Mother with continual multiple frivolous pleadings they know she does not have resources to continue to defend,” Conley wrote.Bridges’ original petition described Johnson’s actions as part of a “deliberate campaign of terror” and accused her of cyberstalking both him and members of his legal team. He further alleged she impersonated a pregnant woman to contact his attorney and trigger a paternity test request.A judge in Mecklenburg County granted the restraining order earlier this month, ordering Johnson to remain 500 yards away from Bridges, his home and team facilities.The legal fight has also expanded beyond the restraining order itself. Bridges recently requested a psychological evaluation for Johnson, alleging what court filings described as “erratic behavior” and an “obsessive fixation” on his current relationship. Johnson has denied those claims as well.The history between the two continues to shadow every new development. Back in 2022, Bridges pleaded no contest to a felony domestic violence charge connected to an incident involving Johnson. The NBA later suspended him for 30 games, while Bridges completed counseling, parenting classes and probation requirements before returning to the court.
Article Summary: Miles Bridges and his ex-partner Mychelle Johnson are once again at the center of a growing legal dispute. Days after Bridges secured a temporary restraining order, Johnson formally responded to several allegations made against her. Johnson denied claims tied to cyberstalking, harassment, and alleged attempts to damage the Charlotte Hornets forward's vehicle.
